rules_evaluated.evidence: line:12: '- **Use** Claude Code to generate a new agent that extends your system'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: LO names Claude Code only; Cowork users do the same move. Maintainer TODO (line 77-78) already flags this — runtime-fork or neutralise the LO so it reads 'use the runtime' rather than naming one path. Not blocking because the TODO is explicit and the claim is true for Code users; but the LO ships a false ceiling for Cowork.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit — write 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
CLAUDE.md describes the runtime, not just the task.
Cloud/remote features carry a Git dependency — out of scope for training dir.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: SharePoint/OneDrive shared deliberation folder is correctly placed in the maintainer block (lines 102-112) as a runtime note with an open dry-run item (line 110: 'Exact OneDrive/SharePoint sync timing with 10-20 concurrent writers'). Not presented to students as a verified capability.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:8: 'Then read Risto on acting on the future and building hypotheses' — named practitioner, first name only, no URL, no source-type label.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add last name, a specific work title (or URL), and a [source-type] label; or rephrase as a conceptual reading direction without attributing to a named person if the reference can't be sourced. Non-blocking: this is a prework reading directive, not a quantitative claim, and no load-bearing number is present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label — mandatory (Rumelt, Martin, Klein/Kahneman references).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rumelt's 'Good Strategy Bad Strategy', Roger Martin's 'what would have to be true?', and Klein/Kahneman's pre-mortem are well-established named works/concepts cited as thinking disciplines — not AI research claims. No quantitative or factual assertions hang on them. Rule 7 vision/detail split applies: these are vision-layer named moves, not detail-layer practitioner deployment claims. PASS on this narrow basis.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rule 11 triggers on 'named practitioner + specific URL + load-bearing number'. This file has named practitioners but zero URLs and zero load-bearing numbers in student-facing content. Trigger conditions not fully met; no source-verification block required.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The Debrief prompt encodes all the forcing mechanics (path-confirm-before-rewrite, two-stalls minimum, one-underspec-role, one-converged-too-fast proposal, before-and-after quote). Body prose around it is descriptive framing, not a forcing function.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The Debrief uses a body-paragraph lead-in pattern (consistent with M2 Debrief and other Agents 101 Debrief prompts), which writing class passed. The paragraph describes the prompt's intent before the **Prompt** label. This is accepted curriculum convention for Debrief sections.
'Builder Claude' is an explicitly enumerated valid destination in check_prompts rule 3. No 'copy →' prefix, no '(end of prompt)' closer. Shape is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No skill invocations in any prompt block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:47: '**Prompt** *(Builder Claude)*' — single unfenced prompt with no runtime-fork divs. LO on line 12 names Claude Code only. Agents 101 is dual-runtime; the Debrief prompt may behave identically across runtimes (no runtime-mechanical difference), but the training is dual-runtime and this has not been confirmed.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Verify the Debrief prompt body is runtime-neutral (no subagent/agent vocabulary split, no plan-mode reference). If it is, one unfenced block with shared label is fine — document that explicitly in the maintainer block. If not, add rt-code/rt-cowork divs. Non-blocking: the TODO on line 77-78 already flags the runtime question for this module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Agents 101-specific).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The Debrief prompt uses 'Identify the file path and confirm in one line before rewriting; overwrite in place' — Claude identifies the file from context rather than the prompt naming a destination. This is intentional: the file being sharpened is the most load-bearing from the session, not a fixed artifact. No naked-filename violation.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Use Claude Code to generate a new agent that extends your system
Module 8 reads the full agent system, the Module 7 next-step file, and the sponsor challenge
Agents that read it. Skills that audit the agents. Judges that catch the skills drifting.

citations_checked.claim: Risto on acting on the future and building hypotheses
Rumelt on crux / Good Strategy Bad Strategy
Three thinking-disciplines from Rumelt, Roger Martin, and Klein and Kahneman
